title: koeleria cristata subsp. mongolica
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. mongolica, also known as Mongolian hair grass, is a species of grass native to the mountainous regions of Mongolia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Koeleria cristata subsp. mongolica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
